note:
数以百万计的美国人生活在宽带沙漠中，无法接入任何类似现代互联网服务的东西。但很少有人像那些必须依赖美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T；)古老的DSL网络的人那样糟糕。
来自密西西比州雷蒙德市的凯西·麦克纳米(Kathie McNamee)是AT&&T DSL的不幸客户之一。McNamee说，她每月向AT&amp；支付35美元购买一项768kbps的互联网套餐，这种套餐几乎不会运行得足够好，以至于她、她的丈夫和两个十几岁的儿子都可以使用。McNamee在读到一篇关于AT&amp；T错误地声称密西西比州的某些家庭可以接入宽带，而实际上AT&amp；T并不能为这些地址提供服务的报道后，联系了ARS。
自2015年以来，AT&amp；T已经从联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)获得了超过2.83亿美元的资金，用于将家庭互联网服务扩展到密西西比州超过13.3万个潜在客户地点。美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)表示，它将在2020年底的最后期限前超过这一要求，但该公司的地图错误给那些认为他们会获得现代宽带的客户带来了令人不快的惊喜。
麦克纳米和她的丈夫大约在两年前买下了他们的房子。她告诉ARS，AT&amp；T事先告诉他们，他们可以获得大约5 Mbps的U-Verse互联网服务。以现代标准衡量，这是缓慢的，但这将比AT&T最终提供的产品要好得多。
技术人员走到这里，他正在做他的事情，他说，首先，你没有资格使用U-Verse。它在这里并不存在，麦克纳米告诉Ars。看着他正在进行的测试，他说，你不会得到5 Mbps的。我甚至不知道他们为什么要卖给你这个。他说，你能得到1 Mbps就很幸运了。
这是因为通往McNamee家的旧电话线距离AT&amp；T节点太远，不符合提供比基本DSL更快的光纤到节点服务的资格。
麦克纳米说：我打电话给美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)，与他们周旋，向我兜售一些卖不到的东西。在他们的地图上，我有资格获得所有这些东西，但我没有资格。[我告诉他们]，我需要你更改你的地图，因为我知道你正在接受联邦资金，说你正在为所有这些地方提供服务，但你却无法做到这一点。AT&amp；T地图后来被更改--在AT&amp；T；的在线服务检查工具中输入McNamee的地址，现在会弹出一条消息，说家里没有互联网服务。
应对可怕的互联网服务一直是一个挑战，特别是在大流行期间。McNamee说，AT&amp；T DSL非常不稳定，这家人甚至很少使用它来完成基本的任务，比如浏览网页。她说：我想要一个家庭安全系统，或者是一个车库门，我可以远程打开，以防我或我的孩子被锁在门外，但互联网连接还不够好。
麦克纳米说，即使是AT&Amp；T的手机服务在他们家里也不可靠。外面必须非常晴朗，没有雨，没有风，没有任何东西，我们可以时不时地把AT&amp；T手机作为移动热点，她说。
麦克纳米的丈夫有时不得不在周末开车50英里去上班，以处理一些如果他们有良好的互联网连接就可以在家里处理的事情，她说。他们十几岁的儿子在完成家庭作业方面也有类似的问题。甚至在家中发送电子邮件也是危险的，这导致学校在没有收到电子邮件作业的情况下出现了问题。
如果他们必须使用电脑，我的大女儿住在麦迪逊县，他们有(康卡斯特)Xfinity，所以我带着我的儿子们去她家玩一天，让他们做任何需要实际电脑工作的事情。麦克纳米说，他们可以在那里工作，然后从那里通过电子邮件发送信息。
在疫情流行期间，孩子们坐在学校、图书馆和麦当劳门店外使用Wi-Fi的故事很常见，麦当劳地区也不例外。她说，有些孩子会去学校，坐在停车场里使用Wi-Fi。
她说，Netflix和其他流媒体服务不在家里工作。麦克纳米说，他们每月支付约250美元购买DirecTV的卫星视频服务，该服务也是AT&Amp；T旗下的。
麦克纳米的家距离密西西比州最近的州际公路大约0.3英里，我们这条街上有12栋房子。她说：大部分地方都是乡下，但我们并不是躲在某个洞里。雷蒙德大约有2500名居民。
她说：我们要求的不是5G、纽约或加州的覆盖范围，而是像样的服务，这样我们的孩子就可以在家做作业了。
McNamee还试图获得AT&amp；T；的固定无线服务，该公司正在利用这项服务来满足其同意的网络扩展要求，以换取FCC的资金。但麦克纳米说，他们的家离AT&amp；T；的手机发射塔太远，无法获得无线家庭互联网服务。麦克纳米说，她采访过的邻居也同样对缺乏宽带感到沮丧。AT&amp；T DSL是唯一可用的……。我们每个人都有同样的问题，她说。
note_en:
Millions of Americans live in broadband deserts with no access to anything resembling modern Internet service. But few people have it as bad as those who must rely on AT&T's ancient DSL network.
 Kathie McNamee of Raymond, Mississippi is one of those unlucky AT&T DSL customers. McNamee said she pays AT&T $35 a month for a 768kbps Internet plan that rarely works well enough to be usable for her, her husband, and two teenage sons. McNamee contacted Ars after reading a story about AT&T  incorrectly claiming that certain homes in Mississippi had access to broadband when in fact AT&T isn't capable of providing service to those addresses.
 AT&T has received over $283 million from the Federal Communications Commission since 2015 to extend home-Internet service to over 133,000 potential customer locations in Mississippi. AT&T says it will exceed that requirement by the end-of-2020 deadline, but the company's mapping mistakes have led to unpleasant surprises for customers who thought they'd get modern broadband.
 McNamee and her husband bought their house about two years ago. She told Ars that AT&T told them in advance they could get U-verse Internet service of about 5Mbps. That's slow by modern standards, but it would have been a lot better than what AT&T ultimately provided.
 "The technician comes out here and he's doing his thing and he said, 'first of all, you're not eligible for U-verse. It doesn't exist out here,'" McNamee told Ars. "Looking at the tests he was running, he said, 'you're not going to get 5Mbps. I don't even know why they would sell you that.' He said, 'you'll be lucky to get 1Mbps.'"
 That's because the old telephone lines leading into McNamee's house are too far from AT&T nodes to qualify for fiber-to-the-node service that provides faster speeds than basic DSL.
 "I called AT&T and went around and around with them selling me something that wasn't available," McNamee said. "They had me on their map as eligible for all of these things, but I'm not eligible. [I told them], 'I need you to change your map because I know you're taking federal funding and saying you're servicing all of these places and yet you're not able to.'" The AT&T map was later changed—typing McNamee's address in AT&T's online service checker tool now brings up a message that says no Internet service is available at the house.
  Coping with horrible Internet service has been challenging, especially during the pandemic. AT&T DSL is so inconsistent that the family rarely even uses it for basic tasks like Web browsing, McNamee said. "I want a home security system or maybe a garage door I can remotely open in case I or my children get locked out," but the Internet connection isn't good enough, she said.
 Even AT&T cellular service is unreliable at their house, McNamee said. "It has to be crystal clear outside, no rain, no wind, no anything, and from time to time we can use" AT&T phones as mobile hotspots, she said.
 McNamee's husband sometimes has to drive 50 miles to work during the weekend to take care of things that could be handled at home if they had a good Internet connection, she said. Their teenage sons have similar problems with completing homework. Even sending emails from home is dicey, which has resulted in problems at school when emailed assignments weren't received.
 "If they have to use a computer, my oldest daughter lives in Madison County and they have [Comcast] Xfinity, and so I take [my sons] to her house for the day and let them do anything that requires actual computer work. They can do their work there and email it from there," McNamee said.
 Stories of children sitting outside  schools,  libraries, and McDonald's stores to use Wi-Fi have been common during the pandemic, and McNamee's area is no exception. "There were children who would go to the school and actually sit in the parking lot" to use Wi-Fi, she said.
 Netflix and other streaming services don't work at their home, she said. McNamee said they pay about $250 a month for DirecTV's satellite video service, which is also owned by AT&T.
 McNamee's house is about three-tenths of a mile from the nearest state highway, MS 18. "There's 12 houses on our street. It is rural for the most part but we're not secluded in a hole somewhere," she said. Raymond has about 2,500 residents.
 "We are not asking for 5G, New York or California coverage, but decent service so our kids can do school work at home," she said.
 McNamee also tried to get AT&T's fixed-wireless service, which the company is using to meet the network-expansion requirements it agreed to in exchange for FCC funding. But their home is too far from AT&T's cellular tower to get the wireless-home Internet service, McNamee said. McNamee said that neighbors she has spoken to are similarly frustrated by the lack of broadband availability. AT&T DSL is "the only thing that's available... every one of us has the same issue," she said.
title: 2020年使用AT&T的768kbps DSL会是什么样子-是的，这很糟糕
title_en: What using AT&T’s 768kbps DSL is like in 2020—yes, it’s awful

note:
Facebook和Twitter正在努力遏制唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)支持者在网上日益高涨的愤怒情绪，因为围绕这位美国总统关于选举被窃取的说法形成了一波势头。
在过去的24小时里，Facebook启用了紧急措施，以使用户更难分享含有误导性信息的帖子，从人们的新闻馈送中删除此类帖子，并限制与投票相关的直播流的传播。
这家社交媒体公司还将其警告标签从政治候选人的帖子扩大到更广泛的右翼影响力人士网络，其中许多人都在呼应特朗普的信息，称它会根据个人的帖子是否在网上疯传来标记他们。
周四下午，Facebook采取行动关闭了一个名为“阻止偷窃”(Stop The Step)的群，该群在短短一天内就聚集了超过36万名关注者。Facebook表示，它对该群的一些成员呼吁采取暴力行动感到担忧。
此举引发了该组织创始人、支持特朗普的美国优先女性组织(Women For America First)的强烈抗议，她们还抱怨称，她们在该组织电子邮件服务MailChimp上的账户已被暂停。“这就是社交媒体对待黑人生命也是抗议者的方式吗？”该组织发言人克里斯·巴伦(Chris Barron)在Twitter上写道。
另一个名为Stand Up Michigan to Unlock Michigan的7.9万人私人组织也被Facebook关闭，但前提是该组织成功呼吁其成员冲击底特律的一个统计中心。
Facebook前首席信息安全官、斯坦福大学(Stanford University)教授亚历克斯·斯塔莫斯(Alex Stamos)说，虽然“在选举日没有什么真正站稳脚跟”，但自那以来，网上的叙述已经“巩固”了几个主题，特别是“停止窃取”，这是一个包罗万象的口号，指的是所有针对民主党人和选举官员的选民欺诈指控。
根据BuzzFeed发布的一份报告，Facebook追踪“暴力和煽动趋势”的内部指标在过去五天里上升了45%，这表明该平台上的不满和紧张情绪日益加剧。Facebook并未否认这一报告。
纽约大学斯特恩商学院(NYU Stern)商业与人权中心(Center For Business And Human Rights At NYU Stern)副主任保罗·巴雷特(Paul Barrett)承认，Facebook和其他平台在这次选举中“更加积极”地给帖子贴上标签，并移除潜在危险的网络群组。
但他表示，在接下来的几天里，Facebook将需要“做得更多、更快”，并呼吁自动化系统“捕捉到这些东西”。
与此同时，在特朗普前首席策略师史蒂夫·班农(Steve Bannon)呼吁处决安东尼·福奇(Anthony Fuci)博士和联邦调查局(FBI)局长克里斯托弗·雷(Christopher Wray)后，Twitter永久禁止了他的账户。
这些举动激怒了特朗普，他在Twitter上指责该平台“失去了控制，这是得益于政府赠送的第230条”，这项法律赋予社交媒体集团对其托管的用户生成内容的豁免权。
推特的一位发言人还表示，该组织“自周二上午以来一直在积极监控#StopTheSteal和相关推文”，并在一些高调的共和党人发布的推文中贴上了警告标签，这些推文推动了这一说法。
这一做法与谷歌(Google)旗下的YouTube形成了鲜明对比，YouTube在这次选举期间因未能对错误信息做出回应而受到抨击。
例如，该公司拒绝撤下右翼媒体集团第一美国新闻网(One America News Network)发布的一段视频，该视频包括特朗普总统赢得选举的虚假说法，以及民主党人进行的选民欺诈指控。
YouTube表示，这段名为“特朗普获胜”的视频包含“明显的虚假内容，破坏了人们对民主进程的信任”--但他补充说，这仍然没有以任何方式违反其政策。

note_en:
Facebook and Twitter are struggling to contain rising anger from Donald Trump’s supporters online, as a wave of momentum builds around the US president’s claims that the election is being stolen from him.
 In the past 24 hours, Facebook has invoked emergency measures to make it harder for users to share posts that contain misleading information, to remove such posts from people’s newsfeeds, and to restrict the circulation of poll-related livestreams.
 The social media company has also expanded its warning labels from posts by political candidates to a wider web of rightwing influencers, many of whom were echoing Mr. Trump’s messages, saying that it was flagging individuals based on whether their posts were going viral.
 On Thursday afternoon, Facebook moved to shut down a “Stop the Steal” group, which had amassed more than 360,000 followers in just a day, saying that it was concerned about calls for violence from some of the group’s members.
 The move prompted outcry from the group’s founders, the pro-Trump Women for America First, who also complained that their account on MailChimp, the group emailing service, had been suspended. “Is this how social media treated Black Lives Matter protesters?” Chris Barron, a spokesperson for the group, wrote on Twitter.
 Another 79,000-strong private group called Stand Up Michigan to Unlock Michigan was also shut down by Facebook, but only after it had successfully called for its members to storm a Detroit counting center.
 Alex Stamos, Facebook’s former chief information security officer and a professor at Stanford University, said that while “nothing really stuck on election day,” online narratives had since “consolidated” around several themes, particularly “Stop the Steal,” a catch-all slogan for all accusations of voter fraud against Democrats and election officials.
 In one sign of the growing discontent and tension on the platform, an internal metric at Facebook that tracks “violence and incitement trends” rose 45 percent over the past five days, according to a report by BuzzFeed that Facebook did not deny.
 Paul Barrett, deputy director of the Center for Business and Human Rights at NYU Stern, acknowledged that Facebook and other platforms had been “much more aggressive” in this election in labelling posts and removing potentially dangerous online groups.
 But he said in the coming days, Facebook will need “to do a lot more and a lot sooner,” and called for automated systems to “catch these things.”
 Twitter, meanwhile, permanently banned the account of Steve Bannon, Mr. Trump’s former chief strategist, after he called for the execution of Dr Anthony Fauci and FBI Director Christopher Wray.
 The moves angered Mr. Trump, who took to Twitter to accuse the platform of being “out of control, made possible through the government gift of Section 230,” the law that grants social media groups immunity for the user-generated content they host.
 A Twitter spokesperson also said the group had been “proactively monitoring #StopTheSteal and related Tweets since Tuesday morning,” adding warning labels to a number of tweets from high-profile Republicans pushing the narrative.
 The approach contrasted with that of Google-owned YouTube, which has come under fire during this election for failing to respond to misinformation.
 In one example, the company refused to take down a video posted by rightwing media group One America News Network that included false claims that President Trump won the election and allegations of voter fraud conducted by Democrats.
 YouTube said that the video, entitled “Trump won,” contained “demonstrably false content that undermines trust in the democratic process”—but added that this still did not breach its policies in any way.

title: Facebook和Twitter努力应对特朗普支持者在网上的愤怒
title_en: Facebook and Twitter struggle with online fury from Trump supporters

note:
在他们的历史上，人类驯养了大量的动物，有些作为食物，有些作为同伴和保护者。一些物种--比如兔子和豚鼠等动物--已经在这两个类别之间进行了部分转换，目前它们既是食物，也是宠物。但有一种物种已经完全抛弃了它作为食物来源的过去。而且，在另一种罕见的情况下，它最终与其说是一种伴侣，不如说是一种装饰品。
我们在这里谈论的是金鱼，现在我们已经看到了它们的基因组。而且它几乎和鱼本身一样怪异。
值得停下来想一想，它们在驯化的领域里是多么的奇怪。它们一开始只是一种完全用于水产养殖的鲤鱼的略带颜色的变体。我们已经将它们从食物链中彻底移除，把它们变成了宠物，但它们不是那种我们可以与之互动的宠物，比如狗或猫，甚至是豚鼠。在很大程度上，他们只是坐在那里，看起来像是装饰性的。在让它们变得更具装饰性的过程中，我们培育出了许多功能远远不如鱼类的品种。
(我邀请你们所有人举一个我没有想到的物种的例子，它有着同样不同寻常的轨迹，并在评论中让我知道。)。
这里也有一段奇怪的历史。虽然我们很笼统地称它们为金鱼，但我们所拥有的大部分并不是真正的金鱼。在中国驯化后(后来从花园池塘转移到室内水缸)，金色的鱼最终留给了皇帝，所以它们仍然相当稀有。与此同时，我们培育出了多条尾巴的品系，没有背鳍的品系，以及更多的品系。
这很可能会在基因上对这种鱼造成一些奇怪的影响。但事实证明，他们一开始就很奇怪。
即使是报告基因组的过程也被证明是有点奇怪的。这最早是在5月份报道的，当时一个研究小组描述了一种金鱼的基因组，并将其与其祖先普通鲤鱼进行了比较。但这项分析相当简约。本周，一个大型财团不仅放弃了对一种金鱼品种的分析，还放弃了对185种不同品种的金鱼的分析。外加16种不同的野生鲤鱼基因组进行比较。虽然金鱼基因组只有18亿个碱基对长(1.8千兆碱基)，但完成这一切所需的原始序列已经达到4.3万亿个碱基。这是一项惊人的努力。
但由于其他研究小组已经发表了这些数据，研究人员在《美国国家科学院院刊》(PNAS)上发表了这一数据，采用的方法只是通过非正式的同行评审。这篇论文似乎没有任何问题会导致同行评议失败，但出版商通常希望获得新奇的结果，而这显然不够新。
大多数动物都有两组相似的染色体，父母各有一组。人类有23条染色体，每条染色体都有两条，这意味着我们每个人都携带46条染色体。金鱼和鲤鱼都有25条染色体，但每条鱼都携带100条染色体，而不是两条，而是四条，或者更确切地说是两条一条。显然，产生鲤鱼的血统是两个密切相关的血统(可能是分离但关系密切的物种)的杂交。
因此，除非基因的某些拷贝因突变而被删除或禁用，否则这种鱼应该有四个拷贝。但在某些特定的情况下，它们并不存在，比如DNA修复基因，其中一组拷贝已经被消除。在许多组织中，一组基因或另一组基因更活跃，但没有明显和一致的模式来确定哪一组基因是活跃的。因此，我们还没有真正了解鱼的四组基因到底发生了什么，但答案不太可能是简单的。
这些鱼是最近才被分离出来的，而且经过了非常严格的挑选，因为它们有着不同寻常的特征--只要看看维基百科上的金鱼品种名单上的图片就知道了。这些生理特征背后的许多遗传变异很可能是最近的，并已被选为该菌株中唯一存在的变异。这就产生了所谓的选择性扫荡，在这种情况下，变种以及它出现时恰好在它附近的任何其他变种，都是种群中唯一存在的变种。
因此，研究人员对这些鱼进行了选择性扫描，不出所料地发现了相当多的鱼。前1%的可能扫描总共包含近1000个基因。斑马鱼是一种与鲤鱼没有太远亲缘关系的物种，其中173个基因已经被删除。携带这些缺失基因的鱼在色素沉着和体型等特征上发生了变化，这正是你所期待的，因为金鱼和大多数其他鲤鱼不同。也有一些与行为有关的变化，但重要的是不要把它们看作狗一样的行为--在鱼身上，更重要的是它们如何进食或对气味的反应。“。
研究人员还仔细研究了蛋金鱼的基因组，这种鱼不再形成背鳍。他们总共确认了近400个与背鳍缺失有关的基因。奇怪的是，大多数变异都来自两个祖先基因组中的一个。而当斑马鱼(57条斑马鱼)中这些基因的子集被敲除后，四分之一的斑马鱼背鳍附近发生了明显的变化。剩下的那些是不是有一个更微妙的问题，是以其他方式造成了差异，还是仅仅是虚假的，目前还不清楚。
虽然目前的分析还不完整，但大量的菌株和序列意味着这里有足够的数据让研究人员忙碌很长一段时间。然而，由于每个基因最多有四个拷贝，对这些动物进行遗传学研究从来都不是一件简单的事情，所以理解序列差异意味着什么将需要相当多的工作。
在某种程度上，这一观点证明了大多数遗传学家专注于斑马鱼的决定是合理的，斑马鱼可能没有金鱼那么多的品系，但确实有更简单的遗传学。不过，作者认为这两个物种可能有亲缘关系。由于每个基因都多了一对拷贝，金鱼可能比斑马鱼能容忍更多的突变。
note_en:
Humans have domesticated a large number of animals over their history, some for food, some as companions and protectors. A few species—think animals like rabbits and guinea pigs—have partly shifted between these two categories, currently serving as both food and pets. But one species has left its past as a food source behind entirely. And, in another rarity, it ended up serving not so much as a companion but as a decoration.
 We're talking goldfish here, and we've now gotten a look at their genome. And it's almost as weird as the fish themselves are.
  It's worth stopping for a moment to consider just how weird they are within the realm of domestication. They started out just as slightly colored variants of a carp that is otherwise used entirely for aquaculture. We've completely removed them from the food chain and turned them into pets, but they're not the sort of pets that we interact with like a dog or cat, or even a guinea pig. Largely, they just sit there and look decorative. And in the process of making them even more decorative, we've bred a lot of varieties that are far less functional as fish.
 (I invite you all to come up with an example of a species I'm not thinking of that has had an equally unusual trajectory and let me know in the comments.)
 There's also a bit of odd history here, too. While we call them goldfish pretty generically, most of what we have are not the actual golden goldfish. After their domestication in China (and later move from garden ponds to indoor tanks), gold-colored fish ended up reserved for the emperor, so they're still fairly rare. In the meantime, we've bred strains with multiple tails, strains that lack dorsal fins, and more.
 That's likely to do some weird stuff to the fish, genetically. But it turns out they were pretty weird to start with.
 Even the process of reporting the genome turned out to be kind of odd. It was first  reported back in May, when a group described the genome of a goldfish and compared it to its ancestor, the common carp. But the analysis was pretty minimalistic. Then, this week, a huge consortium dropped an analysis of not only a strain of goldfish but 185 different strains. Plus 16 different wild carp genomes for comparison. While the goldfish genome is only 1.8 billion base pairs long (1.8 Gibases), the raw sequence required to do all of this ran out to 4.3 trillion bases. It's an astonishing effort.
 But because some other group published the data already, the researchers published it in PNAS using a route that only puts it through informal peer review. There doesn't seem to be anything problematic with the paper that would cause it to fail peer review, but publishers typically want novel results, and this apparently wasn't new enough.
  Most animals have two sets of similar chromosomes, one each from their mother and father. In humans, there are 23 chromosomes, and we have two of each, meaning we each carry 46 of them. In both goldfish and the carp they were derived from, there are 25 chromosomes, but each fish carries 100 of them—instead of two copies, they have four, or rather two sets of two. Apparently, the lineage that produced the carp is a hybrid of two closely related lineages (possibly separate but closely related species).
 Consequently, unless some copies of the genes have been deleted or disabled by mutation, the fish should have four copies of them. But there are some specific cases where they don't, such as DNA repair genes, where one set of copies has been eliminated. And in a lot of tissues, one or the other set of genes is more active, but there's no obvious and consistent pattern of which of the sets it is. So we're not at the point where we really understand what's happening with the fishes' four sets of genes, but the answer is not likely to be simple.
 The fish were only isolated recently and have undergone pretty serious selection for unusual features—just check out the pictures in Wikipedia's  list of goldfish strains. Many of the genetic variants underlying these physical traits are likely to be recent and have been selected as the only variant present in the strain. This creates what's called a "selective sweep" in which the variant, and any others that happen to be near it when it arose, are the only ones present in a population.
 So the researchers checked the fish for selective sweeps and unsurprisingly found quite a number of them. The top 1 percent of possible sweeps contained a total of almost 1,000 genes. In zebrafish, a species that's not too distantly related to carp, 173 of these genes had been deleted. Fish carrying these deletions had changes in features like pigmentation and body shape, which is exactly what you'd expect given the differences between goldfish and most other carp. There were also some changes related to behavior, but it's important not to think of them in terms of dog-like behaviors—in fish, it's more a matter of how they feed or their response to odorants.
  The researchers also looked carefully at the genomes of egg goldfish, which no longer make a dorsal fin. They identified a total of nearly 400 genes that were associated with the loss of the dorsal fin. Oddly, most of the variants were from one of the two ancestral genomes. And when the subset of those had been knocked out in zebrafish (57 of them), a quarter had an obvious change near the dorsal fin. Whether the remaining ones have a more subtle issue, contribute to the difference in some other way, or are simply spurious isn't clear.
 While the current analysis is incomplete, the large number of strains and sequences means there's enough data here to keep researchers busy for a long time. With up to four copies of every gene, though, doing genetics on these animals is never going to be simple, so understanding what the sequence differences mean will take considerably more work.
 To some extent, that idea justifies the decision of most geneticists to focus on zebrafish, which may not have as many strains as goldfish but do have simpler genetics. Still, the authors suggest those two species might be related. With an extra pair of copies of every gene, it may be that the goldfish tolerated far more mutations than the zebrafish could.
title: 驯养鱼类的奇怪基因组
title_en: The weird genomes of domesticated fish

note:
经过数年的等待，一批新的电池电动汽车即将在美国上市。福特正在准备推出野马马赫-E(Mustang Mach-E)。大众汽车(Volkswagen)正在为ID.4做最后的润色。上个月在比利时根特，沃尔沃开始生产XC40充电器。它是其广受欢迎的XC40跨界车的全电动版本，与北极星2(Polestar 2)共享动力总成技术和安卓汽车(Android Automotive)操作系统。
美国的交付还需要几周的时间，所以正式的第一次驾驶，我们花一天时间购买一辆新车，就像今年夏天我们在北极星2上所做的那样，需要等待更长的时间。但本周早些时候，我有机会花了一点时间讲述沃尔沃向我们这些担任世界汽车大奖(World Car Awards)评委的人提供的一个投产前的例子。弗吉尼亚州马纳萨斯的马路是我的游乐场，那里没有公关人员，只要求一个小时后把它带回来。这是我学到的东西。
从外观上看，XC40充电器看起来和其他沃尔沃XC40很像--你现在看到的全电动版本最容易泄露的就是消隐的前部，它比敞开的格栅更有利于提升车身的阻力系数。从驾驶座上可以看到更多的线索。12.3英寸的主仪表显示屏与其他Volvos的主题不同，包括吸引人的新全屏地图模式。在中间一层，信息娱乐屏幕从9英寸增长到11.2英寸，虽然基于磁贴的用户界面看起来很像沃尔沃斯(Volvos)运行的Scount，但它现在运行的是安卓汽车(Android Automotive)。谷歌的语音识别功能意味着它能理解你问的大多数问题，而且谷歌地图驱动的导航应用足够智能，可以知道你的电池电量状态，如果你在路上需要充电，它可以指引你充电。
这款车也没有开始按钮--就像特斯拉Model 3或大众ID.4一样，你只需把它放在研发中，然后开走就行了。任何关于XC40充电器动力系统的疑虑都会在你这么做的那一刻立刻消除。每个车轴都由一个相同的150kW(201hp)、330 Nm(243磅英尺)的永磁式交流电机发电机组驱动，电池组能够为他们两个提供足够的电力，一次给车提供300kW(402hp)的电量。
实际上，它是你真正注意到的660 Nm(486磅英尺)的扭矩，这在很大程度上是因为它到达时的即时性。事实上，这感觉是我开过的最快的沃尔沃，尽管它比瑞典设计的S60 T8或XC60 T8等同样强大的插电式混合动力车重几百磅。对于长凳赛车手来说，它将在4.7秒内完成0到60英里的时速，比关系密切的北极星2慢了十分之几，但比汽油车XC40快了1.5秒。
你可以把XC40充电宝4824磅(2188千克)的重量归咎于电池组--比内燃版多1000磅。它是一个78kWh的包，其中75kWh是可用的，内部使用LG化学袋电池制造。EPA的官方续航里程估计尚未公布，目前沃尔沃只是表示，根据欧洲的WLTP测试制度，它的续航里程将在249至260英里(400至418公里)之间。但考虑到我们的测试车的试生产性质和有限的时间，我还不舒服地评论XC40充电的续航效率。
不过，我更愿意描述自己的驾驶体验。没有不同的驱动模式，只是可以选择前进(D)或后退(R)。打开或关闭单踏板驱动需要向下进入汽车设置的几个菜单级别--如果我有自己的选择，我希望能够像使用变速箱选择器从D转到B来增加插电式混合动力Volvos的再生刹车一样。
尽管它的车身重量略显笨重，但它的乘坐性能很好，悬架也抑制了我寻找的坑洼。较低的重心还可以最大限度地减少车身在弯道的翻滚，尽管直立的驾驶姿势提醒你，尽管XC40充电速度很快，但它在设计时并没有考虑到圈速。如果不是因为日常生活的琐碎，它应该会出类拔萃。毕竟，它带走了我们对XC40汽油车的一切喜爱，然后又增加了上世纪90年代超级跑车的加速能力，同时也缓解了人们对气候变化的良知。53,990美元(扣除7500美元的美国国税局税收抵免之前)并不便宜，但我认为它实际上是很划算的，因为它融合了瑞典风格、跨界实用和电动性能。
note_en:
After what seems like years of waiting, a bevy of new battery-electric vehicles is about to go on sale here in the US. Ford is readying the  Mustang Mach-E. Volkswagen is putting the finishing touches to the ID.4. And last month in Ghent, Belgium, Volvo started production of the XC40 Recharge. It's an all-electric version of its popular  XC40 crossover, one that shares its powertrain technology and Android Automotive operating system with the Polestar 2.
 US deliveries remain a few weeks away, so a proper first drive, where we spend a day with a new car  like we did with the Polestar 2 this summer, will have to wait a little longer. But earlier this week, I got a chance to spend a little time with a pre-production example that Volvo made available to those of us who serve as jurors for the  World Car Awards. The roads of Manassas, Virginia, were my playground, and there was no PR minder, just a request to bring it back after an hour. Here's what I learned.
 From the outside, the XC40 Recharge looks a lot like any other Volvo XC40—the easiest giveaway that you're looking at an all-electric version is the blanked-off front that does better things for one's drag coefficient than an open grille. From the driver's seat, there are a few more clues. The 12.3-inch main instrument display has a different theme from other Volvos, including an attractive new full-screen map mode. In the center stack, the infotainment screen has grown from 9 inches to 11.2 inches, and although the tile-based UI looks pretty similar to Volvos running Sensus, it's now running Android Automotive. Google's voice recognition means it understands most of the questions you ask it, and the Google Maps-powered navigation app is smart enough to know your battery state of charge so it can direct you to charging if needed  en route.
  There's also no start button—like a  Tesla Model 3 or  Volkswagen ID.4, you just put it in R or D and then drive off. Any doubts about the XC40 Recharge's powertrain are immediately dispelled the moment you do. Each axle is driven by an identical 150kW (201hp), 330Nm (243lb-ft) permanent magnet AC motor-generator unit, and the battery pack is able to supply both of them with enough juice to give the car all 300kW (402hp) at once.
 Actually, it's the 660Nm (486lb-ft) of torque you really notice, in large part because of the immediacy with which it arrives. In fact, this feels like the quickest Volvo I've driven, even though it's a few hundred pounds heavier than the similarly powerful plug-in hybrids from Sweden like the  S60 T8 Polestar Engineered or the  XC60 T8. For the bench racers out there, it will do 0 to 60mph in 4.7 seconds, a couple of tenths slower than the closely related Polestar 2 but more than a second and a half quicker than the gasoline XC40.
 You can blame the battery pack for the XC40 Recharge's 4,824lbs (2,188kg) curb weight—about 1,000lbs more than the internal combustion version. It's a 78kWh pack, 75kWh of which are useable,  built in-house using LG Chem pouch cells. The official EPA range estimate has yet to be revealed, and for now Volvo is just saying that it will be "200+ miles"; under the European WLTP testing regime it has scored at between 249 to 260 miles (400-418km). But given the preproduction nature of our test car and the limited time we had, I don't feel comfortable opining on the XC40 Recharge's range efficiency yet.
  I am more comfortable describing the driving experience, however. There are no different drive modes, just a choice of forward (D) or reverse (R). Toggling one-pedal driving on or off requires going down a couple of menu levels into the car's settings—if I had my druthers, I'd like the ability to engage it the same way you can increase regenerative braking in plug-in hybrid Volvos by going from D to B with the transmission selector.
 Despite its slightly lardy curb weight, it rides well, and the suspension damps out the potholes I sought out. The low center of gravity also minimizes body roll through the corners, although the upright driving position reminds you that even though the XC40 Recharge is quick, it wasn't designed with lap times in mind. But for the cut-and-thrust of everyday life, it should excel. After all, it takes everything we loved about the gasoline XC40, then adds 1990s supercar-levels of acceleration while also easing one's conscience about climate change. $53,990 (before the $7,500 IRS tax credit) is not inexpensive, but I think it's actually good value for this mix of Swedish style, crossover utility, and electric performance.
title: 沃尔沃XC40电动汽车充电：第一次驾驶印象
title_en: The electric Volvo XC40 Recharge: First-drive impressions

note:
旧金山选民以压倒性多数通过了一项投票措施，对任何支付给最高薪酬员工的薪酬超过员工中位数100倍的公司征收额外税收。
周二，投票问题获得65%的选民的支持，230,298票赞成，123,943票反对。正如投票问题所说，新税将对旧金山的企业征收，当他们薪酬最高的管理员工的收入超过旧金山员工薪酬中值的100倍时。
这项税收预计每年将筹集6000万至1.4亿美元。大企业-那些总收入超过10亿美元，在全国拥有1000名员工，在旧金山设有行政办公室的企业-将额外缴纳旧金山工资支出的0.4%至2.4%的税款。其他按总收入而不是工资支出缴税的企业将支付旧金山总收入的0.1%至0.6%的额外税款。
NBCNews写道，这项税收不仅影响到像Salesforce这样的大型本地公司，也影响到在这座城市做生意的大公司，比如Visa和摩根大通。NBCNews的文章称，俄勒冈州波特兰也有类似的税收，只影响上市公司，但旧金山的税收既影响私营公司，也影响上市公司。
收取的百分比将根据高管薪酬在最高薪酬和中值薪酬之间的比例大小而有所不同。市政府领导人敦促选民批准这项税收，他们写道：
高管与员工之间的不平等程度越高，附加费就越高。企业可以通过向高管支付更少的工资或提高员工工资来避税。
我们相信，那些有能力支付高管每年百万美元薪水的大公司，有能力支付他们公平份额的税款，以帮助我们恢复元气。在过去的30年里，美国高管的工资飙升了940%。但是正式员工的工资只增长了11%。L号提案激励公司投资于他们的员工，而不仅仅是他们的高管。
市政府领导还表示，新的税收将允许市政府雇佣数百名护士、医生和急救人员。但收入可能每年都会有很大不同。伦敦金融城主管本·罗森菲尔德(Ben Rosenfield)写道，预期支付人的基数较窄，高管薪酬价值和形式的年度波动，以及与增税相关的潜在搬迁风险，都导致了拟议税收的高度波动性，而基于前几年活动的估计可能无法预测未来的收入。罗森菲尔德解释说，他的收入估计为每年6000万至1.4亿美元。
就税收而言，补偿包括工资、薪金、佣金、奖金和包括股票期权在内的财产，以及向直通实体所有者提供服务的补偿，以及支付给员工的任何其他形式的服务报酬。
经济政策研究所(Economic Policy Institute)的一项研究发现，2019年，美国最大的350家企业的首席执行官的收入是普通工人的320倍，高于2018年的293比1，以及1965年的21比1和1989年的61比1。这些数字是基于CEO薪酬的衡量标准，即在授予时计入股票奖励，在兑现时计入股票期权，而不是在授予时计入股票期权。(注：美国经济政策研究所(Economic Policy Institute)的一项研究发现，2019年，美国最大的350家企业的首席执行官的收入是普通员工的320倍，而2018年是293比1,1965年是21比1,1989年是61比1。)。
员工的工资是根据经验和对公司的价值而定的。反对党在共和党政客里奇·格林伯格(Richie Greenberg)撰写的官方声明中表示，通过像L提案那样对高管薪酬征收如此离奇的骗局税，雇佣新的入门级员工(或由于新冠肺炎的业务变化而对现有员工进行再培训)的动机将会减弱。换句话说，作为对这一措施的回应，如果该措施获得通过，公司将减少或停止雇佣低级别员工。此外，这样的税收很可能会阻止新企业迁往旧金山的吸引力，而此时我们正看到疫情引发的前所未有的经济衰退。
格林伯格还指责市政厅领导人对社交媒体和信息技术的痴迷是不健康的，忽视了旧金山不仅仅是社交媒体技术。
他写道，许多市政厅领导人的观点是，科技行业造成了经济失衡，但这些领导人忽视了一个事实，即最先进的先进医学研究设施和金融服务行业在这座城市产生了巨大的影响和员工基础。
这座城市的民主党反驳说，不要相信共和党的恐吓策略。旧金山是美国最受企业欢迎的城市之一。公司税中的一小部分--0.01%--对那些能够负担得起首席执行官每年数百万美元薪酬的公司影响很小，甚至没有影响。
这项税收也遭到了旧金山纳税人协会和旧金山自由党的反对。
投票问题的成功表明，选民们要求我们对不平等问题采取行动，马特·黑尼(Matt Haney)在推特上写道。黑尼是纽约市监事会成员，也是投票问题的发起人。新的收入将支持我们的健康和公共卫生系统，这些系统深受不平等后果的影响。他写道：我们将雇佣护士、社会工作者和急救人员，扩大准入和治疗。
title: 科幻小说中的“多付高管税”打击了那些给CEO支付的薪酬比员工高100倍的公司
title_en: “Overpaid Executive Tax” in SF hits firms that pay CEOs 100X more than workers

note:
在过去的几年里，圣地亚哥市的路灯已经变得智能，配备了一系列摄像头和传感器，可以报告城市及其居民的数据。在隐私维权人士的抗议之后，市长下令暂时关闭网络-但事实证明，在不让城市陷入字面上的黑暗的情况下，市政府工作人员还无法关闭摄像头。
据圣地亚哥之声报道，今年秋天，数以千计的路灯摄像头本应被禁用，但没有软件开关来实现这一目的。当时负责摄像头的供应商并没有关闭摄像头，而是简单地切断了该市对这些设备的网络访问。
智能路灯项目始于五年前的2015年，当时圣地亚哥市长凯文·福尔科纳(Kevin Faulconer)宣布与通用电气照明公司(GE Lighting)达成新的合作伙伴关系，部署一项软件定义的照明技术，帮助圣地亚哥解决该市基础设施方面的一些挑战。
正如圣地亚哥市政府描述的那样，圣地亚哥最初计划改用LED路灯，并加入了一个布满传感器和摄像头的联网数字基础设施。超过3000个具有传感器功能的智能灯被安装在城市的各处(地图)，他们收集的信息(理论上是匿名的)被认为可以帮助城市获得用于城市规划目的的有价值的数据，比如交通管理。
如果说我们从Amazon Ring学到了什么，那就是执法部门倾向于将联网摄像头网络视为一个过于丰富、不容忽视的目标。圣地亚哥也是如此：自2018年以来，当地警方至少使用了全市监控网络的录像400次。
隐私权和民权倡导者推迟到2019年。去年12月，一个名为San Diegans for Open Government的组织起诉该市，称该市未能提供有关智能路灯计划的信息或记录，违反了法律。
今年7月，圣地亚哥市议会一致暂停了对智能路灯项目的资助，福尔科纳在9月下令关闭该网络，直到该市领导层能够起草管理此类监控技术使用的法令。
圣地亚哥最初的合同是与通用电气照明公司(GE Lighting)签订的。然而，通用电气在过去几年一直在四处剥离业务，以帮助管理截至2017年积累的惊人数额的公司债务。
通用电气开发的CityIQ；Smart City平台于今年5月落入一家名为Ubiquia的公司名下，具体金额不详。今年9月，一个城市委员会本应与Ubiquia签署一份为期四年的新合同，但在市长办公室宣布暂停该项目后，该委员会在最后一刻放弃了这项协议。然而，Ubiquia无法切断摄像头，因为它们与灯本身共享相同的电源。
据圣地亚哥之声报道，Ubiquia首席执行官伊恩·亚伦告诉市政府官员，如果你想让我们创建一个软件交换机，我们可以编写定制代码并进行部署。这些单元最初并不是为此而设计的。
摄像机仍在收集素材，这些素材会保留五天，然后被自动删除。市政府让美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)停用了与摄像头的数据连接，这样它们仍在连接的镜头就无法传输到它的服务器上。
note_en:
Over the past few years, streetlights in the city of San Diego have become "smart," equipped with a slate of cameras and sensors that report back data on the city and its denizens. Following protests from privacy activists, the city mayor ordered the network disabled for the time being—but it turns out that city staff can't turn the cameras off just yet without plunging the city into literal darkness.
 Thousands of streetlight cameras were supposed to be disabled this fall, the Voice of San Diego  reports, but there is no software switch for doing so. In lieu of disabling the cameras, the vendor responsible for them at the time instead simply cut off the city's network access to the devices.
 The Smart Streetlight project began five years ago, in 2015, when San Diego Mayor Kevin Faulconer announced ( PDF) a new "partnership" with GE Lighting to deploy "a software-defined lighting technology that will help San Diego solve some of the city's infrastructure challenges."
 As the city government  describes the proposal, San Diego's initial plan to convert to LED streetlights "evolved" to include a "connected digital infrastructure" full of sensors and cameras. More than 3,000 of the sensor-enabled smart lights were installed all around the city ( map), and the information they collect, theoretically anonymous, is supposed to help the city gain valuable data used for urban planning purposes, such as traffic management.
  If there's anything we've learned from  Amazon Ring, it's that law enforcement tends to view networks of connected cameras as too rich a target to ignore. So, too, in San Diego:  local police used footage from the citywide surveillance network at least 400 times since 2018.
 Privacy and civil rights advocates  pushed back through 2019. A group called San Diegans for Open Government  sued the city last December, alleging that the city was violating the law by failing to provide information about or records from the Smart Streetlight Program.
 The San Diego City Council unanimously  suspended funding for the Smart Streetlight program in July, and Faulconer in September  ordered the network to be shut down until such time as city leadership could craft ordinances governing the use of such surveillance technology.
  San Diego's original contract was with GE Lighting. GE, however, has been  divesting businesses left and right in the past few years to help manage a frankly staggering amount of corporate debt it had accumulated by 2017.
 The CityIQ "smart city" platform GE developed  went to a firm called Ubicquia in May of this year for an undisclosed sum. In September, a city committee was supposed to sign a new four-year contract with Ubicquia, but instead it dropped the deal at the last minute when the mayor's office announced suspension of the program. Ubicquia, however, could not cut the cameras, because they share the same power supply as the lights themselves.
 According to reporting by  Voice of San Diego, Ubicquia CEO Ian Aaron told city officials, "If you want us to create a software switch we can write custom code and deploy it. The units were not designed for this originally."
 The cameras are still collecting footage, and that footage is retained for five days before being automatically deleted. The city had AT&T deactivate the data connection to the cameras instead so that the footage they  are still connecting cannot travel to its servers.
title: 尽管有指令，圣地亚哥的间谍路灯仍然亮着
title_en: San Diego’s spying streetlights stuck switched “on,” despite directive

note:
对于纯内燃机驱动的汽车来说，时间已经不多了，汽车业也知道这一点。本周，英国奢侈品的堡垒宾利(Bentley)成为最新一家为这一天设定日期的OEM-2030年。在迈入第二个世纪之际，该公司公布了一项名为Beyond 100；的新计划，称该计划将重塑其业务的方方面面，成为一家端到端的碳中性组织。
宾利已经推出了插电式混合动力电动汽车版的奔驰(Bentayga)SUV，并计划明年在其花名册上再增加一对混合动力电动汽车-想必是大陆GT轿跑车和飞驰(Fly Sur)轿车。2025年，该公司计划推出一款电池电动汽车；宾利首席执行官阿德里安·霍尔马克在接受奥托维克采访时表示，你必须选择一个时间点，让电池功率密度成为我们的解放者，尤其是对较大的汽车而言。我们一直说，2020年代中期，你可以预期供应链上会有120多千瓦时的电池供应。
2025年也将是你能买到不带插头的宾利的最后一年，因为在2026年，该品牌将放弃除混合动力车和BEV之外的所有车型。到2030年，这些混合动力汽车也将消失，只剩下BEV自豪地佩戴着带翼的B徽章。在此过程中，宾利还承诺减少工厂对环境的影响，并实现塑料中立。
开发这款新的Bev应该不会太难，因为宾利将能够利用奥迪(Audi)和保时捷(Porsche)正在开发的新的PPE电动汽车平台(与本特利一样，这两家公司都属于大众集团(Volkswagen Group))。在我做出预测的同时，我要说的是，宣布Beyond 100计划应该会平息有关大众集团(VW Group)将出售宾利(Bentley)的传言。
note_en:
Time is starting to run out for vehicles powered purely by internal combustion engines, and the auto industry knows it. This week Bentley, that bastion of British luxury, became the latest OEM to set a date for that happening—the year 2030. As the company moves into its second century, it has revealed a new plan called "Beyond 100" that it says will "reinvent every aspect of its business to become an end-to-end carbon neutral organization.
 Bentley already introduced a plug-in hybrid EV version of the Bentayga SUV and next year it plans to add another pair of PHEVs to its roster—presumably the Continental GT coupe and Flying Spur sedan. In 2025, the company plans to introduce a battery electric vehicle;  Bentley CEO Adrian Hallmark told Autoweek that "you've got to pick a point in time where battery power density, especially for bigger cars, is the liberator for us. We've always said that the mid-2020s is the time when you can expect to see 120-plus kilowatt-hour batteries coming through the supply chain."
 2025 will also be the last year you'll be able to buy a Bentley that doesn't plug in, because in 2026 the brand is dropping everything other than PHEVs and BEVs. In 2030, those PHEVs will be gone, too, leaving just BEVs to wear the winged B badge with pride. Along the way, Bentley is also pledging to reduce its factory's environmental impact and go plastic neutral.
 Developing that new BEV shouldn't be too hard, as Bentley will be able to take advantage of  the new PPE electric car platform being developed by Audi and Porsche (which, like Bentley, are both owned by Volkswagen Group). And while I'm prognosticating, I'd say that the announcement of the Beyond 100 plan should put paid to  rumors that VW Group is putting Bentley up for sale.
title: 宾利将在2030年前淘汰内燃机
title_en: Bentley will ditch internal combustion engines by 2030

note:
披露：TechCrunch在发布前得到了微软和索尼的游戏机，以及一些来自第一方和第三方出版商的游戏。
按照精心设计的(并且正在进行的！)。针对不同功能和游戏的一系列禁令，关于新平台的印象已经陆续流传了一个月。对于一款已经缺乏冲击力的产品来说，这可能会进一步削弱激动人心的情绪：当任何人都能写到的只是游戏机本身的外观，或者是套装游戏的第一级时，很少有游戏玩家会感到兴奋。有些功能甚至在发布前是不可用的，或者在发布后很长一段时间才会被禁止覆盖，这让评论家们想知道，第一天的改变是否会让他们的印象过时。(当有新的信息出现时，我会更新这篇评论，或者链接到未来的报道。)。
但无论如何，枷锁终于被解除了，现在我们可以谈论一下新游戏机提供的大部分(但不是全部)功能。不幸的是这是…。没那么多。尽管这些公司试图炒作下一代是一个巨大的飞跃，但在发布时根本没有证据表明这一点，而且很可能在几个月内也不会。
这并不意味着新的平台是失败的，甚至也不意味着它们不是很棒。但新一代和老一代很像，兼容它实际上是PS5和X系列在开场阶段最重要的事情。以下是我可以诚实地告诉你我使用PS5的时间。
PS5看起来很奇怪，但我要说的是：没有人会把它误认为其他游戏机。尽管他们可能会认为这是一个空气净化器。
这个巨大的弧形设备可能不适合任何人的装饰，所以最好是咬住子弹，把它放在显眼的地方(幸运的是，它可以舒适地垂直放置或水平放置在支架上)。我期待着为侧面定制盾牌，让这件事变得不那么突出。
玩游戏时，游戏机相当安静，但你可能希望它离你至少几英尺远，特别是当你要玩一张比正常操作声音大得多的光盘时。
至于表现，真的很难说。我玩过的唯一一款“下一代”(实际上是跨代)游戏是“蜘蛛侠：迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯”(Spider-Man：Miles Morales)，虽然它看起来很棒(下面有更多的印象)，但要对这台机器的计算和渲染印章做出任何实质性的评论是非常困难的。
4K和HDR游戏的前景，以及光线跟踪等先进技术改变游戏外观的前景是令人兴奋的。但首先，你需要一个能够利用这些功能的电视设备，其次，完全诚实地说，它们并不像人们所吹嘘的那样。一台过去几年生产的高品质1080p电视看起来几乎一样好，尽管它不支持杜比电视(Dolby Vision)之类的产品。(我知道是因为我在审查期间买了一台新电视。他们看起来都很棒。)。
加载时间--这是这款设备广受赞誉的定制固态硬盘的一个因素--同样很难评估，尽管在迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯(Miles Morales)中从一个菜单到另一个游戏肯定是快的，快的-旅行得更快，上一场游戏的加载速度也比我常规的PS4快。当然，这种好处在不同的游戏中会有所不同--一些开发者公开宣布了他们的性能提升，而另一些不那么令人印象深刻的开发者可能就是听天由命了。如果没有更多的游戏来感受这款游戏机的性能改进，现在你只能相信索尼的话了。
索尼试图将球推进的一个地方是新的DualSense控制器。
不是在形状和颜色以及光滑、透明的按钮上-这些不是那么热。它给人的感觉就像是一个DualShock，让自己有点松懈，而且我绝对不是“PS”形状的PlayStation按钮的粉丝。这东西摸起来就像一块污垢磁铁。
内置扬声器和麦克风也不行；我很难想象有哪种应用能比耳机更好地满足这些需求，或者完全避免使用。
实际上，最明显和令人印象深刻的升级是触发器，它具有令人难以置信的精确机械阻力，可以服务于各种游戏功能，并激发人们的想象力。
新的触发器连接到一组齿轮上，这些齿轮对你的手指施加实际压力，从非常轻的轻拍到(尽管我还没有经历过)真正把你的手指向后推。
它的射程很宽，可以在扳机射程的任何地方施加压力，产生有趣的效果，比如你扣动扳机时会产生阻力(这在暴力游戏中很明显)，然后当你扣动扳机时，扳机就会发出咔哒声并释放出来。在迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯(Miles Morales)身上，触发器就像一个非常敏感的隆隆声，但也会在你挥杆时给你触觉反馈，告诉你什么时候你已经联系上了，等等。
老实说，我很喜欢。我想玩用得好的游戏。我不想玩没有它的游戏！希望开发者会接受可变阻力触发器，因为它真的能增加体验，如果我没弄错的话，甚至有可能让游戏变得更容易使用。
PS4的界面给人一种简洁的错觉，PS5继续保持了这一点，前进了两步，后退了一步。
首先，将机器的“游戏”和“媒体”部分分开是明智之举。随着OTT应用和流媒体服务的激增，它们占据了越来越多的空间，隔离它们是非常有意义的。
至于游戏方面，它与PS4的相似之处在于，它是一条水平线，你可以点击它，当一款游戏被突出显示时，它会“接管”屏幕，显示背景、最新新闻、成就等。和以前一样，它运行得非常好。
以前，当你按下PlayStation按钮时，你会返回主菜单，暂停你正在播放的任何东西。如果你按住按钮，它会打开一个游戏中的侧边菜单，你可以在其中邀请朋友，关闭游戏机和其他常见任务。
PS5的情况正好相反：长时间按键会让你回到主屏幕，而短按键则会调出游戏中的菜单(现在屏幕底部有一排小图标--不喜欢这一变化)。
游戏中的菜单现在有一个深入的“卡片”系统，虽然理论上很酷，但似乎不会真正起到很大的作用。巨型卡片显示最近的屏幕截图和成就，好友活动，如果开发者启用了它，还会显示关于你当前任务或游戏进度的信息。
例如，在迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯(Miles Morales)，按下停顿键告诉我，我已经完成了22%的附带任务，去营救一只名叫蜘蛛侠(Spider-Man)的杂货店猫，上面有一张我接受它的杂货店的照片。不错，但是如果我以普通的方式暂停，游戏中显示的信息是多余的。此外，这些卡片还可以作为游戏功能的“深度链接”，比如多人游戏、进行中的任务、快速旅行地点，甚至是提示。
索尼在《萨克博伊：大冒险》的视频中展示了这些先进的可能性，但由于这款游戏还没有面世，我还不能说它运行得有多好。更重要的是，我不能代表开发人员做出任何承诺，他们可能会很好地集成系统，也可能不会很好地集成系统。至少它是不错的，但我担心它会被降级为第一方游戏(索尼承诺会有很多游戏)，而且在这方面是可选的。
很难称新的用户界面是对旧用户界面的改进--它是不同的，在某些方面更繁忙，在某些方面更精简。它可能会改善情况的地方是减少组织语音聊天和参加朋友游戏等事情上的摩擦。但这一能力还没有做好发射的准备。
我想要注意的几件事是：根据自己的喜好设置PS5超级容易。我马上下载了我的云存储，里面有一个很棒的新设置页面，用于人们在游戏中经常改变的东西：难度、语言、倒置摄像头和其他一些东西。还有内置的辅助功能选项：屏幕阅读器、聊天记录和其他我无法测试但很高兴看到的好东西。
购买新游戏机的主要原因是要在上面玩新游戏。当开关出现时，人们购买它的一半原因是为了扮演令人难以置信的新塞尔达。遗憾的是，对于索尼和微软的粉丝来说，这次发布会上的选择少得可笑。
正如我上面提到的，唯一及时提供给我以获得任何真实印象的游戏是《蜘蛛侠：迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯》(Spider-Man：Miles Morales)。最近在PS4上完成了它的前身，我可以说，新游戏看起来和玩起来都更好，加载时间更短，灯光更好，建筑更精细，等等。但2018年的蜘蛛侠看起来和玩起来仍然非常棒-这是你在续集中预期的不同之处，而不是一代又一代人的不同。(需要明确的是，PS5版本看起来确实要好得多，只是它不是我们所期待的那种黑夜和白天。)。
就评论而言，我只想说，如果你喜欢第一部，你就会喜欢第二部，如果你没有播放原版，那就先播放它，因为它很棒。我还想把它归功于这款新游戏对多样性的承诺。
但这也将出现在PS4…上。以及Xbox One和X系列…。事实上，明年几乎所有的大型比赛都会是这样。
当然，他们在PS5上的表现和外观都会比PS4更好。但很难说服一个人花500美元来玩下一部《刺客信条》或《地平线：4K HDR的零黎明》，而不是1080p。
与此同时，你只能在PS5上玩的少数几款游戏是为小众玩家准备的。萨克博伊看起来是一个有趣的平台，但很难说是一部轰动一时的游戏；恶魔的灵魂是我本赛季最期待的游戏，但翻拍的一款传奇的、但很少有人玩、难度极高的PS3游戏不会打破销售纪录；仅限在线的赛车格斗皇室游戏--毁灭全明星(Deposal All-Stars)被推迟到2月份，这表明它的表现不佳。
把所有这些加在一起，就排他性而言，选择PS5而不是Xbox Series X，或者至少在2021年选择PS4 Pro，并没有太多的理由。
好消息是，PS5现在无疑是玩大量令人惊叹的PS4游戏的最佳方式。几乎所有的设备都会看起来更好，玩得更好，加载速度更快。索尼甚至承认了这一点，当时他们将上一代最好的十几款游戏与PS5捆绑在一起。老实说，我期待着最终扮演战神(我知道…。别来烦我！)。在这件事上，我不仅仅是刺客的信条：瓦尔哈拉。
不幸的是，我还不能说这些PS4游戏在真正的改进方面有什么可说的。如上所述，这在很大程度上取决于开发人员的支持。但作为一个简单的测试，在PS4上加载中央Yharnam区域大约需要33秒，在PS5上加载16秒(正如你在上面的镜头中看到的，游戏看起来是一样的)。我没有给它们计时，但坊间传闻其他游戏也有进步。
不，那不是打字错误。PS5(我们对其竞争对手Xbox Series X的评论也加入了这一观点)，任何人都不应该出于任何理由匆忙购买游戏机。尤其是因为在接下来的一个月左右几乎不可能买到一台PS5，这使得拆开PS5对于渴望的年轻人来说是一种遥远的可能性。
下一代的力量在我能玩的任何一款游戏中都没有体现出来，虽然即将到来的几款游戏可能会展示它的优势，但这些游戏很可能在其他发布它们的平台上表现得同样好。
也没有任何令人信服的新功能让PS5感觉真正的下一代，可能的例外是可变的阻力触发器(X系列至少有多场比赛暂停，如果有任何游戏可以切换，我会嫉妒的)。在接下来的6-8个月里，PS5将只是玩其他所有人都在玩的游戏的最好方式，或者已经玩了很多年了，但是是4K。就这样!。
索尼(Sony)和微软(Microsoft)急于在今年假期前推出这些游戏机，根本得不到发行商和开发商的支持，这些发行商和开发商让游戏机变得值得拥有。明年晚些时候，随着真正的下一代标题和有意义的独家报道开始出现，这种情况将会改变。一年后，PS5和X系列将成为真正的必备品，因为将会有一些只有他们才能买到的东西。
我不是说给你的孩子买一台PS4 Pro作为圣诞节礼物。我并不是说PS5不是玩游戏的好方法。我只是说，除了一些细微的不同之外，许多玩家甚至没有注意到设置，没有理由现在就跑去买一台PS5。放心地在你的旧PS4上享受最新、最棒的游戏吧，因为你知道，当赛博朋克2077捆绑包在夏天上市时，你将节省50美元。
所以，如果你不能拿到PS5来让你在这个冬天保持娱乐，也不要感到难过--PS4目前对你来说是很好的，而下一代人会懒洋洋地走向它最终会变得优雅的游戏机。
note_en:
Disclosure: TechCrunch was provided consoles from both  Microsoft   and  Sony   ahead of release, as well as a handful of titles from first and third-party publishers.
 In accordance with an elaborate (and ongoing!) series of embargoes for different features and games, impressions have been trickling out about the new platforms for a month now. For a launch that’s already lacking impact, this may have further blunted excitement: Few gamers will get excited when all anyone can write about is the exterior of the console itself, or the first level of the pack-in game. Some features wouldn’t even be available before launch, or are prohibited from coverage until long afterwards, leaving reviewers wondering whether day-one changes would make obsolete any impressions they had. (I’ll update this review when new information comes to light, or link to future coverage.)
  But whatever the case, the shackles are finally removed and now we can talk about most (but not all) the new consoles have to offer. Unfortunately it’s… not that much. Despite the companies’ attempts to hype the next generation as a huge leap, there’s simply no evidence of that at launch and probably won’t be for many months.
 That doesn’t mean the new platforms are a flop — or even that they aren’t great. But the new generation is a lot like the old one, and compatibility with it is actually the biggest thing the PS5 and Series X have going for them for the opening stretch. Here’s what I can tell you honestly about my time with the PS5.
   The PS5 is a strange-looking beast, but I’ll give it this: No one is going to mistake it for any other gaming console. Though they  may think it’s an air purifier.
 The large, curvy device likely won’t fit with anyone’s decor, so it may be best to just bite the bullet and display it prominently (fortunately it sits comfortably vertically or on a stand horizontally). I look forward to getting custom shields for the side to make this thing a little less prominent.
 The console is fairly quiet while playing games, but you’ll probably want it at least a few feet away from you, especially if you’re going to play with a disc, which is much louder than normal operation.
 As for performance, it’s really impossible to say. The only “next-gen” (really cross-gen) game I got to play much of was Spider-Man: Miles Morales, and while it looked great (more impressions below), it’s incredibly hard to make any substantive comment on the machine’s computing and rendering chops.
  The prospect of gaming in 4K and HDR, and of advanced techniques like ray tracing changing how games look, is an exciting one. But in the first place you need a TV setup that’s capable of taking advantage of these features, and in the second — to be perfectly honest, they’re not all they’re cracked up to be. A high-quality 1080p TV from the last couple years will look very nearly as good despite not supporting Dolby Vision or what have you. (I know because I got a new TV during the review period. They both looked great.)
 Load times — a factor of the much-lauded custom SSD in this thing — are similarly hard to evaluate, though certainly going from menu to game in Miles Morales was fast, fast-traveling faster, and the previous game was faster to load than on my regular PS4. This benefit will of course vary from game to game, however — some developers are announcing their performance gains publicly, while others with less impressive ones may just let sleeping dogs lie. Without more titles to get a feel for the console’s performance improvements, right now you’ll have to take Sony’s word on things.
   One place where Sony is attempting to advance the ball is in the new DualSense controller.
 Not in the shape and color and slick, transparent buttons — those are not so hot. It feels like a DualShock that’s let itself go a bit, and I’m definitely not a fan of the “PS” shaped PlayStation button. This thing feels like a grime magnet.
 And not in the built-in speaker and microphone, either; I struggle to think of any application for these that wouldn’t be better served by a headset or avoided altogether.
 What’s actually a clear and impressive upgrade is the triggers, which feature incredibly precise mechanical resistance that serves all kinds of gameplay functions and sets the imagination running.
  The new triggers are connected to a set of gears that impart actual pressure against your fingers, from a very light tap to, presumably (though I haven’t experienced it), actually pushing your fingers back.
 The range is wide and it can impart the pressure anywhere along the trigger’s range, giving interesting effects like (the obvious one in violent games) resistance while you pull a gun’s trigger, which then clicks and releases when it fires. In Miles Morales, the triggers act as a very sensitive rumble, but also give you tactile feedback when you’re swinging, telling you when you’ve made contact and so on.
 Honestly, I love it. I want to play games that use it well. I don’t want to play games that don’t have it! Hopefully developers will embrace the variable-resistance triggers, because it genuinely adds something to the experience and, if I’m not mistaken, even has the potential to make games more accessible.
  The PS4’s interface had the illusion of simplicity, and the PS5 continues that with two steps forward and one step back.
 For one thing, separating out the “games” and “media” portions of the machine is a smart move. As OTT apps and streaming services proliferate, they take up more and more space and it makes perfect sense to isolate them.
  As for the games side, it’s similar to the PS4 in that it’s a horizontal line that you click through, and when a game is highlighted it “takes over” the screen with a background, the latest news, achievements and so on. As before it works perfectly well.
 Previously, when you pressed the PlayStation button, you’d return to the main menu and pause whatever you were playing. If you held down the button, it opened an in-game side menu where you could invite friends, turn off the console and other common tasks.
 The PS5 reverses that: The long press now returns you to the home screen, while a short press brings up the in-game menu (now a row of tiny icons on the bottom of the screen — not a fan of this change).
 The in-game menu now sports an in-depth “card” system that, while cool in theory, seems like one of those things that will not actually be used to great effect. The giant cards show recent screenshots and achievements, friend activity and, if the developer has enabled it, info about your current mission or game progress.
 For instance, in Miles Morales, hitting pause told me I was 22% of the way through a side mission to rescue a bodega cat named Spider-Man, with an image of the bodega where I accepted it. Nice, but it’s redundant with the info presented in-game if I pause in the ordinary way. There’s more to it, though — the cards can also be used as “deep links” to game features like multiplayer, quests in progress, quick travel locations, even hints.
  Sony showed off these advanced possibilities  in a video of Sackboy: The Big Adventure, but since that game isn’t yet available I can’t yet speak to how well it works. More importantly, I can’t make any promises on behalf of developers, who may or may not integrate the system well. At the very least it could be nice, but I’m afraid it will be relegated to first-party games (of which Sony promises many), and be optional at that.
 It’s hard to call the new UI an improvement over the old one — it’s different, in some ways more busy and in some ways streamlined. Where it may improve things is in reducing friction in things like organizing voice chat and joining friends’ games. But that capability wasn’t ready for launch.
 A couple nice things I want to note: Setting up the PS5 to your own preferences is super easy. I downloaded my cloud saves in a minute or two, and there’s a great new settings page for things people often change in games: difficulty, language, inverting the camera and some other things. There are also accessibility options built-in: a screen reader, chat transcription and other goodies I wasn’t able to test but am glad to see.
  The chief reason for buying a new console is to play the new games on it. When the Switch came out, half the reason anyone bought it was to play the fabulous new Zelda. Sadly, the selection at  this launch is laughably thin for both Sony and Microsoft fanboys.
 As I noted above, the only game I was provided in time to get any real impressions (that I’m permitted to write about) was Spider-Man: Miles Morales. Having recently completed its predecessor on PS4, I can say that the new game looks and plays better, with shorter load times, improved lighting, more detailed buildings and so on. But the 2018 Spider-Man still looks and plays very well — this is the difference you’d expect in a sequel, not from one generation to the next. (To be clear, the PS5 version does look considerably better, it’s just not the night and day we’ve been led to expect.)
 As far as a review goes, I’ll just say that if you liked the first, you’ll like the second, and if you didn’t play the original, play it first because it’s great. I also want to hand it to the new game for its commitment to diversity.
 But that will also be coming out on the PS4… and Xbox One and Series X… In fact, almost  all the big games of the next year will be.
 They will, of course, play and look better on the PS5 than the PS4. But it’s a hard sell to tell someone to pay $500 so they can play the next Assassin’s Creed or Horizon: Zero Dawn in 4K HDR rather than 1080p.
 Meanwhile, the few games you can only play on PS5 are for niche players. Sackboy looks to be a fun platformer but hardly a blockbuster; Demon’s Souls is my most anticipated title of the season, but a remake of a legendary but little-played and controller-bitingly difficult PS3 game isn’t going to break sales records; and Destruction All-Stars, an online-only racing battle royale game, got delayed until February, which suggests it’s not playing well.
 Adding them all up there really isn’t much reason in terms of exclusives to pick the PS5 over the Xbox Series X or, at least for 2021, a PS4 Pro.
 The good news is that the PS5 is now without question the best way to play the huge catalog of amazing PS4 games out there. Nearly all of them will look better, play better and load faster. Sony as much as admitted this when they bundled a dozen of the best games from the last generation with the PS5. Honestly, I’m looking forward to finally playing God of War (I know… don’t hassle me!) on this thing more than I am Assassin’s Creed: Valhalla.
  Unfortunately I can’t speak to whether these PS4 games have much to speak of in terms of real improvements yet. As mentioned above, a lot of that depends on support from the developers. But as a simple test, loading the Central Yharnam area in Bloodborne took about 33 seconds on the PS4, and 16 on the PS5 (as you can see in the shots above, the game looks identical). I didn’t time them, but anecdotally other games showed improvements as well.
   No, that isn’t a typo. The PS5 (and I am joined in this opinion by our review of its rival, the Xbox Series X) simply isn’t a console anyone should rush out and purchase for any reason. Not least of which because it will be near-impossible to get one in the next month or so, making the possibility of unwrapping a PS5 a remote one for eager youths.
  The power of the next generation is not much on display in any of the titles I have been able to play, and while a handful of upcoming games may show off its advantages, those games will likely play just as well on the other platforms they’re being released on.
 Nor are there any compelling new features that make the PS5 feel truly next-gen, with the possible exception of the variable resistance triggers (the Series X has multi-game suspension at least, and I’d be jealous if there were any games to switch between). For the next 6-8 months, the PS5 will merely be the best way to play the same games everyone else is playing, or has been playing for years, but in 4K. That’s it!
 The rush by Sony and Microsoft to get these consoles out by the holidays this year simply didn’t have the support of the publishers and developers that make the games that make consoles worth having. That will change late next year as the actual next-gen titles and meaningful exclusives start to appear. And a year from now the PS5 and Series X will truly be must-haves, because there will be things that are only available for them.
 I’m not saying buy your kid a PS4 Pro for Christmas. And I’m not saying the PS5 isn’t a great way to play games. I’m just saying that outside some slight differences that many gamers don’t even have the setup to notice, there’s no reason to run out and buy a PS5  right now. Relax and enjoy the latest, greatest games on your old PS4 in confidence, knowing that you’ll save $50 when a Cyberpunk 2077 bundle goes on sale in the summer.
 So don’t feel bad if you can’t lay your hands on a PS5 to keep you entertained this winter — a PS4 will do you just fine for the present while the next generation makes its lazy way toward the consoles it will eventually grace.

这笔投资由Accel牵头，并得到了印度红杉资本(Sequoia Capital India)和现有投资者Stellaris Venture Partners的帮助。该公司报告说，它现在已经筹集了570万美元，其中包括去年种子前的一轮融资。
该公司创始人兼首席执行官迪帕克·安查拉(Deepak Anchala)表示，尽管销售和营销团队试图瞄准广阔的市场，但大多数情况下，他们与客户的电子邮件和其他形式的沟通都失败了。安查拉在之前的初创公司Eight-Double和Tracxn中担任销售人员，这是安查拉亲身经历过的问题。他相信，有了数据，他可以改进这一点，于是他创办了Slintel，建立了一个工具来提供他在之前的职位上缺失的销售数据。
“我们专注于通过识别购买意向高的人来帮助我们的客户解决(缺乏数据)问题。”因此，我们能够告诉销售和营销团队，例如，谁最有可能购买你的产品或服务，谁最有可能在今天购买你的产品，而不是两个月或六个月后，“Anchala解释说。
他们通过观察可能不明显的信号来做到这一点，但这些信号可以让销售团队了解这些公司的关键信息，以及他们很快就会购买的可能性。他说，每家公司都会留下技术足迹。这可能是来自美国证券交易委员会(SEC)备案文件、年度报告、职位空缺等的数据。
“在当今世界，当一家公司使用某一种产品时，会在网上留下巨大的足迹。”因此，我们的算法所做的是，我们将大约1500万家公司的规模映射到他们使用的所有产品，这些产品来自我们能够识别的不同来源--我们每周都会跟踪这些产品。“。
该公司目前有45名员工，预计到2021年底这一数字将翻一番。在建立公司的过程中，特别是作为一名移民创始人，安查拉希望建立一个多元化和包容性的组织。
他说：“我认为，公司今天取得的关键成功之一就是多元化。我们拥有全球员工队伍，因此我们在美国和印度也有员工队伍，我们希望利用这一点。在下一阶段的招聘中，我们将考虑招聘更多元化的应聘者、更多的女性员工和不同国籍的人。
该公司成立于2018年，去年从隐形状态中脱颖而出，已经积累了100家企业客户，今年大多数客户实际上都加入了进来，因为COVID迫使公司想方设法提高销售流程的效率。
note_en:
The investment was led by  Accel   with help from Sequoia Capital India and existing investor Stellaris Venture Partners. The company reports it has now raised $5.7 million, including a pre-seed round last year.
 Deepak Anchala,   company founder and CEO, says that while sales and marketing teams are trying to target a broad market, most of the time their emails and other forms of communication with customers fall flat. As a sales person in previous startups, Eightfold and Tracxn, this was a problem Anchala experienced first hand. He believed with data, he could improve this, and he started  Slintel   to build a tool to provide the sales data that he was missing in these previous positions.
  “We focus on helping our customers solve that [lack of data] by identifying people with high buying intent. So we are able to tell sales and marketing teams, for example, who is most likely to buy your product or your service, and who is most likely to buy your product today, as opposed to two months or six months from now,” Anchala explained.
  They do this by looking at signals that might not be obvious, but which let sales teams know key information about these companies and their likelihood of buying soon. He says that every company leaves a technology footprint. This could be data from SEC filings, annual reports, job openings and so forth.
 “In today’s world there is an enormous amount of footprint left online when a company uses a certain product. So what our algorithms do is we map that at scale for about 15 million companies to all the products that they’re using from the different sources we are able to identify — and we track it all from week to week,” he said.
 The company has 45 employees today and expects to double that number by the end of 2021. As he builds the company, especially as an immigrant founder, Anchala wants to build a diverse and inclusive organization.
 “I think one of the key successes for companies today is having diversity. We have a global workforce, so we have a workforce in the U.S. and India and we want to capitalize on that. In the next phase of hires we are looking at hiring more diverse candidates, more female employees and people of different nationalities,” he said.
 The company, which was founded in 2018, and emerged from stealth last year, has amassed 100 enterprise customers and has seen most of the customers actually come on board this year as COVID has forced companies to find ways to be more efficient with their sales processes.

note:
上周这个时候，随着美国五大科技公司的盈利和其他软件公司的初步业绩，科技股似乎面临着失去魔力的危险。
该交易所探索初创企业、市场和资金。每天早上在Extra Crunch上阅读，或者每周六订阅Exchange时事通讯。
但后来，本周的反弹开始了，更多的收益结果出来了。总体而言，SaaS和云公司的第三季度数据表现中等，或者至少足够好，在将当前营收倍数与历史正常水平进行比较时，足以保护历史上过高的估值。
对于今年不得不应对经济衰退、融资市场参差不齐、时而不确定、选举周期和其他未知因素的尚未上市的初创企业来说，这是个好消息。以市场反弹和上市公司强劲的盈利为2020年画上句号，应该会给私营软件公司带来进入新的一年的光环，帮助它们进行融资和估值辩护。
当然，还有更多的数据需要公布，市场变化无常，许多SaaS公司将在下个月发布财报，会计日历将与你我追踪年度的方式相差一个月。但在本周与JFrog的首席执行官、BigCommerce的首席执行官和Ping Identity的首席财务官通了一段时间的电话后，我认为一切都很好。
Redpoint的贾敏·鲍尔是一位风险投资家，他在财报季不知不觉地兼职担任交易所(The Exchange)的研究部门。他首先对本周发布的一组SaaS和云计算股票的收益结果进行了综述。正如你可能还记得的，上周我们对它的一系列结果有点不以为然。
正如我们所看到的，在第三季度，小组中只有一次失误。不出所料，这家名为SurveyMonkey的公司也是预计第四季度营收低于市场预期的三家SaaS公司之一。我在这张图表上看到，在进行了超出预期的第四季度业绩预测的公司中，持乐观态度的公司不到80%，第三季度的业绩也是如此，其中包括大量超过目标至少10%的公司。
在这些数据中，有两个我想探究的故事。第一个是与COVID有关的摩擦，第二个是与COVID有关的加速度。世界上每家公司都在经历至少一部分前者。例如，即使是在大流行期间看到产品需求激增的公司，也必须应对一个销售市场，在这个市场上，它们不能按自己希望的方式运营。
据报道，对于软件公司来说，在加速的数字化转型中，问题变成了COVID的缺点是否超过了它的优点。我们将通过本周交易所公布第三季度财报后采访的三家公司的视角来探讨这一问题。
在本周我们的三家公司中，Ping Identity的表现最为艰难；尽管第三季度收益超出预期，但该公司的股价在第三季度业绩下滑后大幅下跌。
该公司营收下滑3%，年度经常性营收(ARR)增长17%。如果它的股价超出预期，为什么它的股价会下跌？你可以读到它的第四季度指引略显软弱。在上面的图表中，它被标记为轻微的节拍，但它的低端低于分析师的预期，这创造了预期失误的可能性。
投资者押注于平安转向SaaS的举措无论是现在还是长期都会带来增值，但他们并没有对平安第四季度的预测感到兴奋。
note_en:
This time last week, in the wake of earnings from tech’s five largest American companies and  early results from other software companies, it appeared that tech shares were in  danger of losing their mojo.
  The Exchange   explores startups, markets and money. Read it  every morning on Extra Crunch, or get  The Exchange newsletter every Saturday.
  But then,  this week’s rally launched, and more earnings results came in. Generally speaking, the Q3 numbers from SaaS and cloud companies have been medium-good, or at least good enough to protect historically stretched valuations when comparing present-day revenue multiples to historical norms.
 This is great news for yet-private startups that have had to deal with a recession, an uneven and at-times uncertain funding market, an election cycle and other unknowns this year. Wrapping 2020 with a market rally and strong earnings from public comps should give private software companies a halo heading into the new year, assisting them with both fundraising and valuation defense.
 Of course, there’s still a lot more data to come in, markets are fickle and many SaaS companies will report next month, having a fiscal calendar offset by a month from how you and I track the year. But after spending time on the phone this week with JFrog’s CEO, BigCommerce’s CEO and Ping Identity’s CFO, I think things are turning out just fine.
   Kicking off,  Redpoint’s Jamin Ball, a venture capitalist who unconsciously moonlights as the research desk for the The Exchange during earnings season, has a roundup of earnings results from this week’s set of SaaS and cloud stocks that reported. As you will recall, last week we were slightly unimpressed by its cohort of results.
   As we can see, there was a single miss amongst the group in Q3. Unsurprisingly, that company,  SurveyMonkey,   was also one of three SaaS companies to project Q4 revenue under street expectations. My read of that chart is seeing a little less than 80% of the group that did project Q4 guidance that bests expectations is bullish, as were the Q3 results, which included a good number of companies that topped targets by at least 10%.
 Inside of the data are two narratives that I want to explore. The first is about COVID-related  friction, and the second is about COVID-related  acceleration. Every company in the world is experiencing at least some of the former. For example, even companies that are seeing a boom in demand for their products during the pandemic must still deal with a sales market in which they cannot operate as they would like to.
 For software companies, reportedly in the midst of a hastening digital transformation, the question becomes whether or not the COVID’s minuses are outweighing its pluses. We’ll explore the matter through the lens of three companies that The Exchange spoke with this week after they reported their Q3 results.
   Of our three companies this week,  Ping Identity   had the hardest go of it; its stock fell sharply after it dropped its Q3 numbers, despite beating earnings expectations for the period.
 The company’s revenue fell 3%, while its annual recurring revenue (ARR) rose by 17%. Why did its stock fall if it came in ahead of expectations? You could read its Q4 guidance as slightly soft. In the above chart it’s marked as a slight beat, but its  low-end came in under analyst expectations, creating the possibility of a projected miss.
 Investors, betting on Ping’s move to SaaS being accretive both now and in the long-term, were not stoked by its Q4 forecast.
title: 软件公司报告的第三季度业绩相当不错。
title_en: Software companies are reporting a pretty good third quarter

note:
这轮融资由安大略省教师养老金计划委员会(Ontario Teachers‘Payment Plan Board)旗下的创新基金TIP牵头，专注于对提供颠覆性技术的公司进行后期风险和成长性股权投资。现有合作伙伴富达中国特殊情况公司(Fidelity China Special Situations PLC)、5Y Capital(前身为晨兴风险投资)、ClearVue Partners和Eight Roads也参与了这轮融资。
据该公司表示，新资金将主要用于研发。
Pony.ai在其存在的四年中赢得了投资者、原始设备制造商和一级供应商的支持。这家在中国和加州运营的公司自成立以来已经筹集了超过10亿美元，其中包括来自丰田的4亿美元。小马与博世(Bosch)、现代(Hyundai)和丰田(Toyota)等汽车制造商和供应商有几个合作伙伴或合作伙伴。
Pony正在打造一款它所说的不可知的虚拟司机，适用于各种大小的车辆，从小型轿车到大型卡车，并在拼车和物流(递送)服务网络上运营。该公司早在2019年就表示，正在与OEM和供应商合作，将其自动化技术应用于长途卡车运输市场。但它最出名的可能是围绕机器人出租车所做的努力。
该公司已经在加利福尼亚州弗里蒙特和欧文以及中国广州推出了拼车和通勤试点。去年，一支配备了Pony.ai和威盛(Via)叫车平台的自动驾驶系统的现代科纳(Hyundai Kona)电动跨界车车队开始在公共道路上接送客户。这项名为BotRide的机器人出租车服务并不是一项无人驾驶服务，因为方向盘后面一直有一名人类安全司机。BotRide试点于2020年1月结束。
然后，该公司开始在欧文地区运营一项名为Pony Pilot的公共机器人出租车服务。随着新冠肺炎疫情席卷全球，小马将机器人出租车服务从载客转变为包裹。今年4月，Pony.ai宣布已与电商平台YamiBuy合作，为欧文的客户提供自主最后一英里的送货服务。Pony.ai当时表示，推出新的快递服务是为了提供额外的运力，以应对新冠肺炎疫情引发的在线订单激增。
note_en:
The round was led by TIP, an innovation fund within the Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board that focuses on late-stage venture and growth equity investments in companies that deliver disruptive technology. Existing partners Fidelity China Special Situations PLC, 5Y Capital (formerly Morningside Venture Capital), ClearVue Partners and Eight Roads also participated in the round.
 The new funds will primarily be used for research and development, according to the company.
  Pony.ai has won over investors, OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers during its four-year existence. The company, which operates in China and California, has raised more than $1 billion since its founding, including $400 million from Toyota. Pony has several partnerships or collaborations with automakers and suppliers, including Bosch, Hyundai and Toyota.
 Pony is building what it describes as an agnostic virtual driver for all sizes of vehicles, from small cars to large trucks, and to operate on both ridesharing and logistics (delivery) service networks. The company said back in 2019 that it was working with OEMs and suppliers to apply its automated technology to the long-haul trucking market. But it’s perhaps best known for its effort around robotaxis.
 The company has launched ridesharing and commuter pilots in Fremont and Irvine, California and Guangzhou, China. Last year, a fleet of electric, autonomous Hyundai Kona crossovers equipped with a self-driving system from Pony.ai and Via’s ride-hailing platform began shuttling customers on public roads. The robotaxi service, called BotRide, wasn’t a driverless service, as there was a human safety driver behind the wheel at all times. The BotRide pilot concluded in January 2020.
 The company then started operating a public robotaxi service called PonyPilot in the Irvine area. Pony shifted that robotaxi service from shuttling people to packages as the COVID-19 pandemic swept through the world. In April, Pony.ai announced it had partnered with e-commerce platform Yamibuy to provide autonomous last-mile delivery service to customers in Irvine. The new delivery service was launched to provide additional capacity to address the surge of online orders triggered by the COVID-19 pandemic, Pony.ai said at the time.
title: 中国自动驾驶汽车初创公司Pony.ai估值达到53亿美元
title_en: Chinese autonomous vehicle startup Pony.ai hits $5.3 billion valuation

note:
以下是今年入围Next40榜单的40家民营企业名单：
但创建一家没有福利的俱乐部是有点没用的。这就是为什么你作为Next40和French Tech 120的成员可以得到一些额外的好处。这些公司会自动进入快速通道管理系统--每个初创公司都有一名代表来满足他们的特定需求。
如果你面临管理问题，比如为外国雇员申请签证，获得认证或专利，或者将你的产品卖给公共管理部门，你可以更容易地找到合适的人来解决你的问题。
“最酷的是，他们可以要求我们做任何事情：‘我即将在中国做bizdev’，‘我要发射火箭，我需要在太空设施上测试它’，或者‘我正在雇佣50人，我需要他们和他们所有的家人在这里，’”La French Tech主管Kat BorLongan去年告诉我。
现在，政府正在努力刷新该指数。为了做到这一点，你必须应用并匹配与去年完全相同的标准。
一旦你证明自己是一家独立的法国初创公司，有两种不同的方式可以被Next40录取：
如果你在过去三年里筹集了超过1亿欧元的资金，或者如果你的公司估值在10亿美元或更高，你就会自动成为下一个40强的一员。
如果你完成了“过去三年中最重要的一轮融资”，并在过去三年中以30%或更高的年增长率创造了超过500万欧元的收入。
如果它们在过去三年的一轮融资中筹集的资金超过2000万欧元，其中40家将被选中。
其中40家将根据年营业额和增长率进行选择。
Next40指数中的一些公司将留在该指数中，其他公司将退出该指数。政府对此无动于衷。
在接下来的几年里，一些公司将关闭，另一些公司将被法国和外国公司收购。这是正常的，也是健康的。“法国数字部长塞德里奇·奥在新闻发布会上说。
有两件新的事情值得强调。首先，政府与泛欧交易所(Euronext)签署了一项合作协议，教育企业家如何上市。法国上市科技公司寥寥无几，政府希望能扭转这一趋势。
其次，2021年1月，政府还将选择20家绿色科技初创企业，以便它们可以获得相同的快车道计划。这将是一份单独的清单。
note_en:
Here’s a list of the 40 private companies in this year’s Next40:
   But creating a club without perks would be a bit useless. That’s why you get some perks as a member of the Next40 and French Tech 120. Those companies automatically access a fast-track administrative system — every startup gets a representative for their particular needs.
 If you’re facing administrative issues, such as getting visas for foreign employees, getting a certification or a patent or selling your product to a public administration, you can more easily find the right person that can solve your issue.
 “The coolest thing is that they can ask us for anything: ‘I’m about to do bizdev in China,’ ‘I’m launching a rocket and I need to test it on a space facility’ or ‘I’m hiring 50 people and I need them and all their families here,’ ”  La French Tech   director Kat Borlongan  told me last year.
 Now, the government is working on refreshing the index. And in order to do that, you’ll have to  apply and match the exact same criteria as last year.
 Once you have proven that you’re an independent French startup, there are two different ways to get accepted in the Next40:
 If you have raised more than €100 million over the past three years or if your company has a valuation of $1 billion or more, you’re automatically part of the Next40.
 If you’ve closed “one of the most important funding rounds of the past three years” and you generate more than €5 million in revenue with a year-over-year growth rate of 30% or more for the past three years.
  40 of them will be selected if they have raised more than €20 million in a funding round over the past three years.
 40 of them will be selected based on the annual turnover and the growth rate.
 Some companies in the Next40 will remain in the index, others will leave the index. And the government is fine with that.
 “In the coming years, some companies will shut down, others will get acquired by French and foreign companies. It’s normal and healthy,” France’s digital minister Cédric O said in a press briefing.
 There are two new things that are worth highlighting. First, the government has signed a partnership with Euronext to educate entrepreneurs about going public. There are few public French tech companies, and the government hopes it can reverse this trend.
 Second, in January 2021, the government will also select 20 greentech startups so they can access the same fast-track programs. It is going to be a separate list.
title: 法国初创企业可以申请Next40和French Tech 120
title_en: French startups can apply to the Next40 and French Tech 120

note:
它现在已经筹集了135万GB的种子期资金，由Connect Ventures牵头，来自AngelList的AI Seed、Moxxie Ventures、Andy Chung和Philipp Moehring，以及Indeed.com联合创始人保罗·福斯特(Paul Forster)的家族理财室的支持下，亚历克斯·罗特(Alex Roetter)(Twitter的前工程副总裁)。
Purple Dot由Skyscanner的资深员工玛德琳·帕拉(Madeline Parra)和约翰·塔尔博特(John Talbott)于2019年8月创立，允许消费者要求更低的价格。零售商的优势在于，他们可以决定是否在季节中期以略微降低的速度发布时尚产品，以确保销售。
顾客仍会预先付款，然后等待商品确认，否则将获得全额退款。紫点支付方式与“先买后付”的融资方式并驾齐驱。
这个“值得等待”的价格通常不会低于推荐零售价的10-20%，从而减少季末折扣造成的损失，因为季末折扣要大得多。对消费者来说，好处是他们不会在购买时累积债务。
这家初创公司表示，它已经在与英国和美国的一些主要商业街品牌进行谈判，但已经获得了男装零售商Speech的支持，后者也将利用这项技术进行“预购”。这意味着他们可以以有限的方式测试新的款式、设计和面料，从而在他们致力于新的服装系列时减少浪费(从而减少碳排放)。
Purple Dot首席执行官马德琳·帕拉(Madeline Parra)评论说：“今天在网上购物时，顾客可以选择支付零售价，也可以选择离开。当他们真的离开时，商品会经过打折过程，对商家来说变得无利可图，并听天由命于垃圾填埋场。这种二元系统对任何人都不起作用--顾客失去了这件商品，因为在他们试图再次购买它之前，它可能会在他们的尺码上脱销，商家也会失去这笔交易。紫点公司提供了一种新的购物方式，同时挑战不可持续的、无情的消费主义、糟糕的定价策略和逐利的销售，从而正面解决了这个问题。
在接受TechCrunch采访时，她还补充说，“与Klarna不同，我们不鼓励消费者购买他们买不起的东西。”
Connect Ventures的普通合伙人皮埃特罗·贝扎(Pietro Bezza)评论说：“紫点公司的创新主张为零售商提供了库存问题的解决方案，从而使他们受益。季末‘恐慌性销售’长期以来一直给零售商带来财务上的不确定性，并对环境造成同等程度的负面影响。“
note_en:
It’s now raised a seed round of £1.35 million, led by  Connect Ventures,   with support from AI Seed,  Moxxie Ventures,   Andy Chung and Philipp Moehring from  AngelList,    Alex Roetter   (former SVP of Engineering at Twitter) and the family office of Paul Forster, co-founder of Indeed.com.
 Founded in August 2019 by senior Skyscanner employees Madeline Parra (CEO) and John Talbott (CTO), Purple Dot allows consumers to request a “worth-the-wait” lower price. The advantage for retailers is that they can then decide whether or not to release a fashion product mid-season at a slightly reduced rate in order to secure the sale.
  The customers still pays upfront and then waits to have the item confirmed, receiving a full refund if not. The Purple Dot payment method sits alongside “buy now, pay later” finance options.
  This “worth-the-wait” price does not usually fall below a 10-20% reduction from the recommended retail price, thus reducing losses from end-of-season discounting, where discounts are much deeper. The advantage for the consumer is that they don’t then rack up debt on their purchases.
 The startup says it’s already in talks with a number of major U.K. and U.S. high street brands but has already secured menswear retailer  Spoke, which will also use the tech for “pre-ordering”. This means they can test out new styles, designs and fabrics in a limited manner, thus reducing waste (and therefore carbon emissions) when they commit to a new line of clothing.
 Madeline Parra, CEO of Purple Dot, commented: “When shopping online today, customers can either pay the retail price or walk away. When they do walk away, the item goes through the discounting process, becomes unprofitable for the merchant and is resigned to landfill. This binary system isn’t working for anyone – the customer loses out on the item, because it may go out of stock in their size before they attempt to purchase it again, and the merchant loses the sale. Purple Dot tackles this problem head-on by providing a new way to shop, taking on unsustainable, unrelenting consumerism, poor pricing tactics and profit-crunching sales at the same time.”
 Speaking to TechCrunch she also added that “Unlike Klarna, we don’t encourage consumers to buy stuff they can’t afford.”
 Pietro Bezza,   general partner at Connect Ventures, commented: “Purple Dot’s innovative proposition benefits retailers by creating a solution to their inventory problems. End of season ‘panic sales’ have long caused financial uncertainty for retailers and a negative impact on the environment in equal measure.”
title: 在Connect Ventures的资助下，Purple Dot计划承担Klarna式的收购债务
title_en: Funded by Connect Ventures, Purple Dot plans to take on Klarna-style purchase debt

note:
疫情蔓延到西方后，互联网平台上充斥着当地质量不明和/或可疑的个人防护用品的广告，以及其他可疑的冠状病毒产品-即使在一些公司禁止了此类广告之后。
这里令人担忧的不仅是消费者被敲诈，而且如果人们购买的产品没有提供声称的防止接触病毒的保护，甚至在实际不存在的情况下被出售虚假的冠状病毒“治疗”，那么真正的伤害风险也是如此。
欧盟司法专员迪迪埃·雷恩德斯(Didier Reynders)今天在一份声明中表示：“我们从早先的经验中了解到，欺诈者将这场流行病视为欺骗欧洲消费者的机会。”我们也知道，与主要在线平台合作对于保护消费者免受非法行为的影响至关重要。今天，我鼓励这些平台联合起来，进行点对点的交流，以进一步加强它们的回应。在目前席卷欧洲的第二波浪潮中，我们需要更加灵活。“。
欧盟委员会表示，雷德斯今天会见了11家在线平台-包括亚马逊、阿里巴巴/全球速卖通、eBay、脸书、谷歌、微软/必应、乐天和(TechCrunch的母公司)威瑞森媒体/雅虎-讨论与疫情有关的新趋势和商业做法，并敦促科技公司采取更多措施阻止新一轮新冠肺炎骗局。
今年3月，欧盟成员国消费者保护当局就这一问题通过了共同立场。自那以来，欧盟委员会和一个泛欧盟消费者保护执法者网络一直与这11个平台保持定期联系，以推动对冠状病毒骗局构成的威胁做出协调反应。
欧盟委员会声称，这一行动已导致平台报告并删除了“数亿”非法优惠和广告。该公司还表示，他们已经证实了它所说的与冠状病毒相关的新上市公司数量“稳步下降”，但没有提供更详细的数据。
下个月，地方立法者将公布一揽子立法，建议更新现有的电子商务规则，并旨在增加他们的法律责任，包括围绕非法内容和危险产品。
在上周的一次演讲中，欧盟委员会负责数字政策的执行副总裁玛格丽特·维斯特格(Margrethe Vestager)表示，数字服务法案(DSA)将要求平台在处理非法内容和危险产品方面承担更多责任，包括通过标准化报告非法内容的流程，以及处理与内容相关的报告和投诉。
下个月也将出台的第二个立法方案--《数字市场法》(Digital Markets Act)--将为被认为占据主导市场地位的平台子集引入额外的规则。这可能包括要求他们向竞争对手提供数据，目的是促进数字市场的竞争。
欧洲议会议员还推动将“了解你的企业客户”的原则纳入DSA。
与此同时，发改委一直在敦促社交媒体平台公开其在6月份所称的冠状病毒“信息性”，以打击与新冠肺炎有关的虚假信息。
今天，欧盟委员会公布了Facebook、谷歌、微软、Twitter和TikTok在9月份为打击冠状病毒虚假信息而采取的最新行动-发布了第三套监测报告。印度国内市场专员蒂埃里·布雷东(Thierry Breton)表示，在这方面也需要做更多工作。
他说：“与疫情有关的虚假信息的病毒传播使我们公民的健康和安全处于危险之中。在接下来的几周里，我们需要与网络平台进行更强有力的合作，以有效地打击虚假信息。“他在一份声明中说。
这些平台是欧盟(不具法律约束力的)虚假信息行为守则的签字国。
针对平台处理非法仇恨言论等内容的具有法律约束力的透明度规则，似乎将成为DSA一揽子计划的一部分。不过，如何处理“有害内容”(如与公共健康危机相关的虚假信息)这一更加模糊的问题仍有待观察。
一项旨在解决虚假信息问题的欧洲民主行动计划也将在今年年底前出台。
在欧盟委员会今天发布的最新监测报告中，负责价值观和透明度的副总裁维拉·朱罗瓦(Vera Jourová)发表了尖锐的评论：“平台必须加紧努力，变得更加透明和负责。”我们需要一个更好的框架来帮助他们做正确的事情。“
note_en:
After the pandemic spread to the West, internet platforms were flooded with local  ads for PPE of unknown and/or dubious quality and other dubious coronavirus offers — even after some of the firms  banned such advertising.
 The concern here is not only consumers being ripped off but the real risk of harm if people buy a product that does not offer the protection claimed against exposure to the virus or even get sold a bogus coronavirus “cure” when none in fact exists.
  In a statement today, Didier Reynders, the EU commissioner for justice, said: “We know from our earlier experience that fraudsters see this pandemic as an opportunity to trick European consumers. We also know that working with the major online platforms is vital to protect consumers from their illegal practices. Today I encouraged the platforms to join forces and engage in a peer-to-peer exchange to further strengthen their response. We need to be even more agile during the second wave currently hitting Europe.”
 The Commission said Reynders met with 11 online platforms today — including Amazon, Alibaba/AliExpress, eBay, Facebook, Google,  Microsoft/Bing,    Rakuten   and (TechCrunch’s parent entity) Verizon Media/Yahoo — to discuss new trends and business practices linked to the pandemic and push the tech companies to do more to head off a new wave of COVID-19 scams.
 In March this year EU Member States’ consumer protection authorities adopted a common position on the issue. The Commission and a pan-EU network of consumer protection enforcers has been in regular contact with the 11 platforms since then to push for a coordinated response to the threat posed by coronavirus scams.
 The Commission claims the action has resulted in the platforms reporting the removal of “hundreds of millions” of illegal offers and ads. It also says they have confirmed what it describes as “a steady decline” in new coronavirus-related listings, without offering more detailed data.
  Next month regional lawmakers are set to unveil a package of legislation that will propose updates to existing e-commerce rules and aim to increase their legal responsibilities, including around illegal content and dangerous products.
  In a speech  last week, Commission EVP Margrethe Vestager, who heads up the bloc’s digital policy, said the Digital Services Act (DSA) will require platforms to take more responsibility for dealing with illegal content and dangerous products, including by standardizing processes for reporting illegal content and dealing with reports and complaints related to content.
 A second legislative package that’s also due next month — the Digital Markets Act — will introduce additional rules for a sub-set of platforms considered to hold a dominant market position. This could include requirements that they make data available to rivals, with the aim of fostering competition in digital markets.
 MEPs have also  pushed for a “know your business customer” principle to be included in the DSA.
 Simultaneously, the Commission has been pressing for social media platforms to open up about what it described in  June as a coronavirus “infodemic” — in a bid to crack down on COVID-19-related disinformation.
 Today the Commission gave an update on actions taken in the month of September by Facebook, Google, Microsoft, Twitter and TikTok to combat coronavirus disinformation — publishing its  third set of monitoring reports.  Thierry Breton, commissioner for the internal market, said more needs to be done there too.
 “Viral spreading of disinformation related to the pandemic puts our citizens’ health and safety at risk.  We need even stronger collaboration with online platforms in the coming weeks to fight disinformation effectively,” he said in a statement.
 The platforms are signatories of the  EU’s (non-legally binding) Code of Practice on disinformation.
 Legally binding transparency rules for platforms on tackling content such as illegal hate speech  look set to be part of the DSA package. Though it remains to be seen how the fuzzier issue of “harmful content” (such as disinformation attached to a public health crisis) will be tackled.
 A European Democracy Action Plan to address the disinformation issue is also slated before the end of the year.
 In a pointed remark accompanying the Commission’s latest monitoring reports today, Vera Jourová, VP for values and transparency, said: “Platforms must step up their efforts to become more transparent and accountable. We need a better framework to help them do the right thing.”
title: 欧洲敦促电商平台共享数据以打击冠状病毒骗局
title_en: Europe urges e-commerce platforms to share data in fight against coronavirus scams

这家创业公司有一个“五维”感官平台，它说，它可以实时地、在视线之外感知、预测和预防车祸。该公司表示，其“事故预防技术平台”将专有视觉传感器、机器学习和雷达与超远距离和预见能力结合在一起，以防止高速和任何天气条件下的碰撞。Provizio团队由机器人、人工智能、视觉和雷达传感器开发方面的专家组成。
Provizio首席执行官巴里·伦恩(Barry Lunn)说：“我们在Provizio所做的一切都是由1/3/5的交通事故造成的。我们组建了一支令人难以置信的球队，这支球队每天都在成长。AI是汽车事故预防的未来，搭载AI技术的Provizio 5D雷达是迈向这一目标的第一步。“。
参与Provizio的还有斯科特·塞耶(Scott Thayer)博士和杰夫·米什勒(Jeff Mishler)教授，他们是卡内基梅隆机器人公司(Carnegie Mellon Robotics)的正式成员，以为谷歌/Waymo、Argo、Aurora和优步(Uber)开发早期自动驾驶技术而闻名。
note_en:
The startup has a “five-dimensional” sensory platform that — it says — perceives, predicts and prevents car accidents in real time and beyond the line-of-sight. Its “Accident Prevention Technology Platform” combines proprietary vision sensors, machine learning and radar with ultra-long range and foresight capabilities to prevent collisions at high speed and in all weather conditions, says the company. The  Provizio   team is made up of experts in robotics, AI and vision and radar sensor development.
 Barry Lunn, CEO of Provizio said: “One point three five road deaths to zero drives everything we do at Provizio. We have put together an incredible team that is growing daily. AI is the future of automotive accident prevention and Provizio 5D radars with AI on-the-edge are the first step towards that goal.”
  Also involved in Provizio is Dr. Scott Thayer and Prof. Jeff Mishler, formally of Carnegie Mellon robotics, famous for developing early autonomous technologies for  Google   /  Waymo,   Argo, Aurora and Uber.
